title: Malveopsis albens
taxonomy: Malveopsis albens belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow family. This classification places Malveopsis albens within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The genus Malveopsis is a part of the larger clade of Malvaceae, which also includes other genera like Malva and Hibiscus. Malvaceae is a member of the order Malvales and is closely related to the families Cistaceae and Tiliaceae. As a member of the Malvales clade, Malveopsis albens shares characteristics with other plants in this group.
uses: Malveopsis albens is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. intro: Malveopsis albens, also known as White Mallow, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Malvaceae. This plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Malveopsis albens include mucilages, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
